preamble: The relationship between [a] and [l] is most commonly seen in the formation of syllabic consonants. In many Germanic languages, an unstressed [a] followed by [l] reduces, effectively turning the liquid into the syllable nucleus.

examples.note: The vowel [ə] (from earlier [a]) is absorbed into the syllabic liquid.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2014). A Course in Phonetics. https://archive.org/details/courseinphonetic00lade
Gimson, A. C. (2014). Gimson's Pronunciation of English. Routledge.
